title: Avoid using DateTime.Now for benchmarking or timing operations
description: Avoid using "DateTime.Now" for benchmarking or timing operationsAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellThe rule targets the use ofDateTime.Nowcall followed by some arithmetic operation.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoUsingDateTime.Nowcalls within a subtraction operation to measure elapsed time is not recommended. This property is subject to changes such as daylight savings transitions, which can invalidate the calculation if the change occurs during the benchmark session, or when updating a timer. Moreover,DateTime.Nowis dependent on the system clock, which may have low resolution on older systems (as low as 15 milliseconds).Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Avoid using DateTime.Now for benchmarking or timing operations. Use Stopwatch or DateTimeOffset.UtcNow for precise timing.
